channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏛️ Lawmakers demand answers about $620M Pentagon loan to firm tied to Trump Jr. | Defense One
item.description: In a letter, a group of Democratic Congress members wrote that ProPublica’s reporting on the deal “reveals a staggering level of corruption and influence.”

item.title: 🔐 Australia’s cybersecurity workforce problem: language that repels the people we need | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Australia has a cybersecurity workforce problem, and part of the explanation is hiding in plain sight: the language of the field actively repels the people we need. Last year, I was on a panel for ...

item.title: 🛡️ The Afghanistan-Pakistan Border Is Still Unstable | Foreign Policy
item.description: There is a brief lull in violence, but the root causes of the conflict haven’t been addressed.

item.title: 🕵️ The Acting DNI and the Intelligence Office Trump Wants | Just Security
item.description: Bill Pulte’s appointment as Acting Director of National Intelligence suggests that ODNI may now be serving a more political function than advising the president. The post The Acting DNI and the Intelligence Office Trump Wants appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 India’s Cockroach Movement Gains Momentum | Foreign Policy
item.description: A satirical political party has tapped into the discontent among unemployed youth.

item.title: 🌍 Iran Launches Deadly Attack on Kuwait’s Main Airport | Foreign Policy
item.description: Experts warn that continued strikes could jeopardize peace talks.

item.title: 🛡️ Experts warn terrorism threat is rising in Africa as US pulls back | Defense News
item.description: A jihadist front is gaining ground across Africa, the Center for Strategic and International Studies warned in its annual risk analysis.

item.title: 🌏 Europe has had enough of China’s export surge | Atlantic Council
item.description: Can advanced industrial democracies preserve manufacturing capacity in the face of state-backed overproduction on a continental scale? The post Europe has had enough of China’s export surge appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌏 New Large Chinese Submarine With Very Unique Feature Just Caught On Satellite Imagery | The War Zone
item.description: The nuclear fast attack-sized mystery submarine could be China's answer to a high-speed interceptor boat. The post New Large Chinese Submarine With Very Unique Feature Just Caught On Satellite Imagery appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🛡️ Trump strips civil-service protections from thousands of feds | Defense One
item.description: Defense, State, DHS positions among those converted to at-will jobs.

item.title: 🏴 Dispatch from Kyiv: Russia is more vulnerable than it might seem | Atlantic Council
item.description: A recent trip to Ukraine reveals a country growing in strength and confidence, while Russia is increasingly on the back foot. The post Dispatch from Kyiv: Russia is more vulnerable than it might seem appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌏 The seabed is becoming a battlefield. Taiwan has field notes | ASPI Strategist
item.description: As Australia strengthens the protection of critical seabed infrastructure, it should draw on Taiwan’s frontline experience. Taiwan has been battling this problem for three years. At the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore on 30 May, Deputy ...

item.title: 📍 What to Know About Ethiopia’s Election | Foreign Policy
item.description: Abiy’s all-but-certain victory threatens to destabilize the African nation.

item.title: 📍 Why Is Trump Targeting Brazilian Gangs? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The U.S. designation of two Brazilian crime groups as terrorist organizations scrambles already tense bilateral relations.

item.title: 🏛️ Air Force awards GE, Rolls-Royce for ‘medium thrust’ drone engines | Breaking Defense
item.description: The two engine makers will advance designs to power medium thrust class drones, an Air Force spokesperson told Breaking Defense.

item.title: 🕵️ Should we be worried about the bond market? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Bond yields have spiked, especially in advanced economies. But why is that? And what does that actually mean? Alice Fulwood, US business editor at the Economist and co-host of the podcast Money Talks, joins Josh and Jessie to decode the signals in the global bond market—from…

item.title: 🏛️ Marine Corps’ Harrier completes final flight following SOUTHCOM deployment | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Marine Corps is moving through its Tactical Aircraft Transition Plan as it plans to fully adopt fifth-generation aircraft across the entire fleet.

item.title: 🕵️ Reading between the lines of Trump’s new executive order on AI | Atlantic Council
item.description: Atlantic Council experts dig into the details of US President Donald Trump’s recently signed executive order on artificial intelligence. The post Reading between the lines of Trump’s new executive order on AI appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 The New Weapon Behind Ukraine’s Battlefield Success | Foreign Policy
item.description: Mid-range drones that can hit targets almost 100 miles behind Russian lines have changed the game.

item.title: 🌏 Will China and the United States Pursue Strategic Stability? | War on the Rocks
item.description: In 2024, Michael Swaine wrote, “How to Stop the United States and China from Sliding into War,” where he identified areas that could increase the possibility of an armed conflict between the United States and China. Two years later, after recent talks between President Trump and…

item.title: 📍 The biggest industry, training hurdles for making drone wingman a reality | Breaking Defense
item.description: In our final video looking at manned-unmanned teaming, we consider likely changes ahead for defense manufacturers as the US increasingly partners with drones in combat.

item.title: 📍 Nature and Concrete: The Eco-Brutalist Resistance in Central Asia | The Diplomat
item.description: Eco-brutalism is an aesthetic appeal to a healthier, greener future, and one that can be built atop existing realities.

item.title: 🏛️ The Hidden History of America’s Harrier Jump Jets | The War Zone
item.description: The Harrier story is a transatlantic saga of improvisation, ambition, and unlikely partnership, which helped shape the F-35B. The post The Hidden History of America’s Harrier Jump Jets appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🔐 Defense Business Brief: Cyber force, outlined; Shipbuilding game; USMC’s JLTV plea | Defense One
item.description: Click to read the full story at the source.

item.title: 🛡️ Uzbekistan’s New Migration Destination? America. | The Diplomat
item.description: From irregular border crossings to bilateral training agreements, Uzbekistan is working to carve out a formal pathway to U.S. employment.

item.title: 🕵️ Trump's choice of acting intel chief threatens fragile surveillance-powers deal | Defense One
item.description: If William Pulte's appointment is not rescinded, Senate Democrats say, it could sink an extension of FISA Section 702.

item.title: 🌍 Anduril teams with Elbit America for Army’s self-propelled howitzer competition | Breaking Defense
item.description: Anduril joins the US subsidiary of Israeli company Elbit along with OshKosh Defense for the team’s offering for the highly anticipated competition.

item.title: 🏴 Russia’s Baltic Fleet Successfully Attacked By Ukrainian Drones | The War Zone
item.description: Once considered a sanctuary for Russian naval power, the Baltic Fleet is now in the crosshairs of Ukraine’s expanding long-range strike campaign. The post Russia’s Baltic Fleet Successfully Attacked By Ukrainian Drones appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Congress Prepares for a Pair of Consequential Iran Votes | Foreign Policy
item.description: Tehran may feel empowered to wait out U.S. demands for concessions in face of war-weary American public.

item.title: 🌏 North Korea Sees a New Solar Power Push | 38 North
item.description: The recent opening of a new solar farm in Haeju, South Hwanghae province, marks a turning point ... The post North Korea Sees a New Solar Power Push appeared first on 38 North.
